title: InterferenceResult Object
description: Represents the interference between bodies and/or occurrences in an interference analysis.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: entityOne
properties.description: Returns the first entity involved in the interference

properties.name: entityTwo
properties.description: Returns the second entity involved in the interference

properties.name: interferenceBody
properties.description: Returns a transient BRepBody that represents the volume of interference.

properties.name: isCreateBody
properties.description: Gets and sets if this interference volume should be created as a model body. Setting this to true doesn't create the body just indicates that a body is desired. Calling the createBodies method on the interferenceResults object will result in the creation of the model body if this property is true.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: InterferenceResult.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

entityOne.title: InterferenceResult.entityOne Property
entityOne.description: Returns the first entity involved in the interference

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = interferenceResult_var.entityOne

related_samples.name: Analyze Interference API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates analyzing the interference between components. This uses a direct modeling design because the ability to create bodies that represent the interference volume is only supported in a direct modeling design.

isCreateBody.title: InterferenceResult.isCreateBody Property
isCreateBody.description: Gets and sets if this interference volume should be created as a model body. Setting this to true doesn't create the body just indicates that a body is desired. Calling the createBodies method on the interferenceResults object will result in the creation of the model body if this property is true.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = interferenceResult_var.isCreateBody

# Set the value of the property.
interferenceResult_var.isCreateBody = propertyValue
